meta.description: The original canvas by Pigneur and Osterwalter (2010).

canvas.title: Key Partners
canvas.subtitle: Who helps us with what?
canvas.description:
Who are our Key Partners? Who are our key suppliers? Which Key Resources are we acquiring from partners? Which Key Activities do partners perform? 

MOTIVATIONS FOR PARTNERSHIPS: Optimization and economy, Reduction of risk and uncertainty, Acquisition of particular resources and activities

canvas.title: Key Activities
canvas.subtitle: How do we create value?
canvas.description:
What Key Activities do our Value Propositions require? Our Distribution Channels? Customer Relationships? Revenue streams?

CATEGORIES: Production, Problem Solving, Platform/Network

canvas.title: Key resources
canvas.subtitle: What are our ingredients to create value?
canvas.description:
What Key Resources do our Value Propositions require? Our Distribution Channels? Customer Relationships Revenue Streams?

TYPES OF RESOURCES: Physical, Intellectual (brand patents, copyrights, data), Human, Financial

canvas.title: Value Propositions
canvas.subtitle: What problem do we solve? Why do our customers buy?
canvas.description:
What value do we deliver to the customer? Which one of our customer’s problems are we helping to solve? What bundles of products and services are we offering to each Customer Segment? Which customer needs are we satisfying?

CHARACTERISTICS: Newness, Performance, Customization, “Getting the Job Done”, Design, Brand/Status, Price, Cost Reduction, Risk Reduction, Accessibility, Convenience/Usability

canvas.title: Customer Relationships
canvas.subtitle: How do we acquire our customers?
canvas.description:
What type of relationship does each of our Customer Segments expect us to establish and maintain with them? Which ones have we established? How are they integrated with the rest of our business model? How costly are they?

TYPES: Personal assistance, self-service, automated services, communities, co-creation

canvas.title: Channels
canvas.subtitle: How do we communicate our value propositions?
canvas.description:
Through which Channels do our Customer Segments want to be reached? How are we reaching them now? How are our Channels integrated? Which ones work best? Which ones are most cost-efficient? How are we integrating them with customer routines?

TYPE: direct and indirect channels

canvas.title: Customer Segments
canvas.subtitle: For whom do we solve problems?
canvas.description: For whom are we creating value? Who are our most important customers? Is our customer base a Mass Market, Niche Market, Segmented, Diversified, Multi-sided Platform

canvas.title: Cost Structure
canvas.subtitle: What do we spend on?
canvas.description:
What are the most important costs inherent in our business model? Which Key Resources are most expensive? Which Key Activities are most expensive?

IS YOUR BUSINESS MORE: Cost Driven (leanest cost structure, low price value proposition, maximum automation, extensive outsourcing), Value Driven (focused on value creation, premium value proposition).

SAMPLE CHARACTERISTICS: Fixed Costs (salaries, rents, utilities), Variable costs, Economies of scale, Economies of scope

canvas.title: Revenue Streams
canvas.subtitle: How do we earn money?
canvas.description:
For what value are our customers really willing to pay? For what do they currently pay? How are they currently paying? How would they prefer to pay? How much does each Revenue Stream contribute to overall revenues?

TYPES: Asset sale, Usage fee, Subscription Fees, Lending/Renting/Leasing, Licensing, Brokerage fees, Advertising

FIXED PRICING: List Price, Product feature dependent, Customer segment dependent, Volume dependent

DYNAMIC PRICING: Negotiation (bargaining), Yield Management, Real-time-Market
